county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1859 from unorganized territory in west-central Lower Michigan. County seat is Muskegon on Lake Michigan. Named from Ojibwe for 'marshy river.' One of Michigan's great lumber cities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Muskegon County Register of Deeds in Muskegon holds land records from 1859 organization. County Clerk has marriage records and court records. Probate Court has estate files. The Muskegon County Museum and Hackley Public Library have genealogical collections. Muskegon was one of Michigan's great lumber cities on Lake Michigan -- Charles Hackley and other lumber barons built the city. Significant African American community from the Great Migration. Dutch, Scandinavian, Polish, and Irish immigrants settled in large numbers. The Lakeshore Museum Center has industrial and ethnic community records. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Muskegon Chronicle newspaper archives.

source_profiles.source_name: Muskegon County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1859 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Muskegon County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1859. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Muskegon County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1859. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.source_name: Muskegon County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Muskegon County from 1860 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.source_name: Muskegon County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Muskegon County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Automobile Industry and Labor Records -- Muskegon County
source_profiles.repository: Walter P. Reuther Library (Wayne State University) / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Automobile manufacturing employment records, union (UAW) records, and industrial documentation for Muskegon County. The Walter P. Reuther Library at Wayne State University holds United Auto Workers records and related labor archives. City directories and employee rosters trace worker families. Essential for 20th-century family research in Michigan's industrial counties.

source_profiles.source_name: Muskegon County Historical Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers for Muskegon County including local weeklies and dailies. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage and death announcements, estate settlements. Michigan Digital Newspaper Archive and Chronicling America have digitized collections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1851 from unorganized territory in west-central Lower Michigan. County seat is White Cloud. Named for a Chippewa chief. Lumber heritage on the Muskegon River.
county_metadata.research_tips: Newaygo County Register of Deeds in White Cloud holds land records from 1851 organization. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Newaygo County Society of History and Genealogy has compiled family files and cemetery records. Lumber industry along the Muskegon River drove early settlement. Brooks Township had early African American settlers. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Archives of Michigan has lumber company records. White Cloud and Fremont newspapers document local families.

source_profiles.source_name: Newaygo County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1851 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Newaygo County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1851. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Newaygo County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1851. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.source_name: Newaygo County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Newaygo County from 1860 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.source_name: Newaygo County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Newaygo County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Local historical and genealogical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Newaygo County churches, township, and family burial grounds. Find A Grave has growing online coverage. Local historical and genealogical societies have published cemetery surveys. Michigan Cemetery Source at the Library of Michigan is a statewide index.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1820 from Macomb County in southeast Lower Michigan. County seat is Pontiac. Named for the Oakland hills. One of Michigan's earliest organized counties and now its wealthiest suburban county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Oakland County Register of Deeds in Pontiac holds land records from 1820, one of Michigan's earliest organized counties. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses from the 1820s and court records. Probate Court has estate files from the territorial era. The Oakland County Pioneer and Historical Society has genealogical collections. Pontiac was an important early settlement and later automobile manufacturing center (Pontiac Motor Division of GM). The Oakland County Research Library has local history materials. Underground Railroad routes passed through Oakland County. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Pontiac Public Library has city directories. Cranbrook Archives in Bloomfield Hills hold significant cultural records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Oakland County Register of Deeds online search for land records from 1820

source_profiles.source_name: Oakland County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Oakland County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1820. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Oakland County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1820. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.source_name: Oakland County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Oakland County from 1827 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and African American Records -- Oakland County
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Burton Historical Collection (Detroit Public Library)
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan was a free state, but the Underground Railroad was active throughout southern Michigan with routes to Canada. Oakland County records include freedom seekers' documentation, abolitionist society records, African American church records, and community histories. The Burton Historical Collection and Archives of Michigan hold related materials.

source_profiles.source_name: Automobile Industry and Labor Records -- Oakland County
source_profiles.repository: Walter P. Reuther Library (Wayne State University) / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Automobile manufacturing employment records, union (UAW) records, and industrial documentation for Oakland County. The Walter P. Reuther Library at Wayne State University holds United Auto Workers records and related labor archives. City directories and employee rosters trace worker families. Essential for 20th-century family research in Michigan's industrial counties.

source_profiles.source_name: Oakland County Tax Rolls -- Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1820 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.

source_profiles.source_name: Oakland County Historical Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers for Oakland County including local weeklies and dailies. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage and death announcements, estate settlements. Michigan Digital Newspaper Archive and Chronicling America have digitized collections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1851 from unorganized territory in west-central Lower Michigan. County seat is Hart. Named for the Great Lakes as 'inland oceans.' Lake Michigan shoreline. Fruit farming county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Oceana County Register of Deeds in Hart holds land records from 1851 organization. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Oceana County Historical and Genealogical Society has compiled family files and cemetery records. Lumber industry drove initial settlement, followed by fruit farming (asparagus, cherries). Pentwater and Shelby developed as Lake Michigan port communities. Bohemian (Czech) immigrants settled near Mears. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Archives of Michigan has supplementary records.

source_profiles.source_name: Oceana County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1851 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

NARA holds Bureau of Indian Affairs records (Great Lakes Agency). Tribal government offices maintain enrollment records. Archives of Michigan has related territorial records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1875 from Iosco County in northeast Lower Michigan. County seat is West Branch. Named from Ojibwe for 'chief.' Lumber and agriculture.
county_metadata.research_tips: Ogemaw County Register of Deeds in West Branch holds land records from 1875 when organized from Iosco County. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Ogemaw County Historical Society in West Branch has local materials. Lumber industry was the primary economic driver along the Rifle River. German settlers were numerous. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Check Iosco County for pre-1875 records. Archives of Michigan has supplementary records. West Branch newspapers document local families.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1896 fire destroyed courthouse and many records
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1861 from unorganized territory in the western Upper Peninsula. County seat is Ontonagon on Lake Superior. Named from Ojibwe. Copper mining heritage. 1896 fire destroyed courthouse (burned county).
county_metadata.research_tips: Ontonagon County Register of Deeds in Ontonagon holds land records, but the 1896 fire destroyed the courthouse and many early records. This is a burned county. Post-fire records restart from 1896. The Ontonagon County Historical Society has reconstructed some genealogical data. Copper mining was the dominant industry -- the famous Ontonagon Boulder (copper mass) drew early attention. Finnish, Cornish, and other immigrant miners settled here. Michigan Technological University Archives has mining company records. Check the Archives of Michigan for surviving pre-fire records and BLM GLO for land patents. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-fire vital records. Finnish American Heritage Center at Finlandia University has Finnish immigrant records.

source_profiles.source_name: Ontonagon County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1896 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens. Some gaps due to 1896 fire destroyed courthouse and many records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1896 fire destroyed courthouse and many records

source_profiles.source_name: Ontonagon County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1896. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1896 fire destroyed courthouse and many records

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1896. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Ontonagon County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Ontonagon County Mining Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Michigan Technological University Archives / Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Employment rolls, payroll records, accident reports, and company town records from copper mining operations in Ontonagon County. Michigan Tech's Copper Country Historical Collections and the Archives of Michigan hold mining company records. Essential for tracing immigrant mining families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1869 from unorganized territory in central Lower Michigan. County seat is Reed City. Named for the Seminole leader Osceola. Railroad junction and lumber heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Osceola County Register of Deeds in Reed City holds land records from 1869 organization. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Osceola County Historical Society has local materials. Lumber industry drove early settlement. Reed City developed as a railroad junction. Irish, German, and Canadian settlers formed early communities. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Archives of Michigan has supplementary records. The Pere Marquette Railroad records document workers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1881 from unorganized territory in north-central Lower Michigan. County seat is Mio. Named from a Schoolcraft coinage. One of Michigan's least populous counties. AuSable River heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Oscoda County Register of Deeds in Mio holds land records from 1881 organization. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. Oscoda County is one of Michigan's least populous counties. Lumber industry drove initial settlement, but the county remained sparsely populated. The AuSable River is a major feature. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Archives of Michigan has supplementary records. Very limited local institutional resources -- state-level repositories are essential.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1875 from unorganized territory in north-central Lower Michigan. County seat is Gaylord. Named from an Iroquois word via New York. Lumber heritage, later resort and winter sports area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Otsego County Register of Deeds in Gaylord holds land records from 1875 organization. County Clerk has marriage records and court files. The Otsego County Historical Museum in Gaylord has local genealogical materials. Lumber industry drove early settlement. The county later developed as a resort and winter sports area. German and Polish settlers were numerous. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records. BLM GLO land patents. Archives of Michigan has supplementary records. Gaylord Herald Times newspaper archives.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1837 from Kent and Mackinac at Michigan statehood. County seat is Grand Haven. Named for the Ottawa (Odawa) people. Dutch immigrant heritage centered in Holland (1847 colony of Albertus Van Raalte).
county_metadata.research_tips: Ottawa County Register of Deeds in Grand Haven holds land records from 1837 organization. County Clerk maintains marriage licenses and court records. Probate Court has estate files. The Holland Museum and Joint Archives of Holland (Hope College, Western Theological Seminary, and Holland Museum) hold exceptional Dutch immigrant records from the 1847 Albertus Van Raalte colony. Dutch Reformed Church (RCA) and Christian Reformed Church (CRC) records are essential for Holland-area families. The Tri-Cities Historical Museum in Grand Haven has local materials. Ottawa County has one of Michigan's strongest concentrations of Dutch heritage. FamilySearch microfilm covers vital records and church records. BLM GLO land patents. The Herrick District Library in Holland has genealogy resources.

source_profiles.source_name: Ottawa County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1837 to present. Michigan is a public land state -- original federal land patents available through BLM GLO. Register of Deeds holds grantor/grantee indexes, deed books, mortgages, and liens.

source_profiles.source_name: Ottawa County Probate Records -- Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, guardianship records, and administration files from 1837. Michigan Probate Courts also handled adoption records, mental health commitments, and name changes.

source_profiles.source_name: Ottawa County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1837. Michigan County Clerks issue marriage licenses. State registration of marriages began 1867. Pre-1867 marriages may also be documented in church records.

source_profiles.source_name: Ottawa County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census for Ottawa County from 1840 to 1950. Michigan territorial censuses (1827, 1834) may include early residents. Michigan state censuses (1874, 1884, 1894, 1904) provide intercensal data.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Michigan is a public land state. Original federal land patents for Ottawa County are available free online at BLM GLO. Cash entry, homestead, and military bounty land warrants. Essential for identifying first landowners.

source_profiles.source_name: Ottawa County Dutch Reformed Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Joint Archives of Holland / Calvin University Heritage Hall / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Reformed Church in America (RCA) and Christian Reformed Church (CRC) records from Dutch immigrant congregations. Baptisms, memberships, marriages, and death records. The Joint Archives of Holland (Hope College) and Calvin University Heritage Hall in Grand Rapids hold extensive Dutch Reformed church records. Essential for tracing Dutch immigrant families who settled in western Michigan from 1847.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Treaty records, tribal census rolls (annuity rolls), allotment records, and agency correspondence for Ottawa/Odawa communities in Ottawa County. NARA holds Bureau of Indian Affairs records (Great Lakes Agency). Tribal government offices maintain enrollment records. Archives of Michigan has related territorial records.

source_profiles.source_name: Ottawa County Tax Rolls -- Archives of Michigan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax rolls listing property owners, assessed values, and personal property. Michigan tax rolls from 1837 at the Archives of Michigan. Essential for tracking land ownership and residency between census years.

source_profiles.repository: Archives of Michigan / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers for Ottawa County including local weeklies and dailies. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage and death announcements, estate settlements. Michigan Digital Newspaper Archive and Chronicling America have digitized collections.
